stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Spain’s annual Las Fallas festival is held each March in the Valencia region.
  • It marks the arrival of spring and the feast day of Saint Joseph.
  • Crowds gather to admire and then burn large, satirical papier-mâché sculptures called fallas.
  • In Valencia, hundreds of fallas are displayed throughout the city streets before being set ablaze.
  • In Gandia, dozens of fallas are displayed throughout the city streets before being set ablaze.
stem: The student wants to emphasize a similarity in how people in Valencia and Gandia celebrate Las Fallas. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: In both Valencia and Gandia, people display fallas throughout the city streets before setting them ablaze.
Hundreds of fallas are displayed in Valencia, whereas only dozens are displayed in Gandia.
In Valencia, hundreds of fallas are displayed throughout the city streets.
Las Fallas marks the arrival of spring and the feast day of Saint Joseph.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence emphasizes a similarity in how people in Valencia and Gandia celebrate Las Fallas: in both cities, fallas are displayed throughout the streets before being set ablaze.

Choice B is incorrect. The sentence emphasizes a difference in numbers rather than a similarity. Choice C is incorrect. The sentence describes Valencia only; it doesn’t mention Gandia and so cannot emphasize a similarity between the two. Choice D is incorrect. The sentence describes the festival’s general meaning, not how people in Valencia and Gandia celebrate it.
